adjudication_basis: Source-grounded, matcher-independent: each 'same' role is identified by its struct-field offset / defining-op semantics, which are identical in both dumps (the rev diff was a GetNameSlot inline extraction that renumbered virtuals but did not change these struct accesses). The three 'li 0' counters (matched igs 35/38/51) are NOT independently adjudicated: they share an identical first-def and are only distinguishable by use-site, which is the matcher's OWN feature, so scoring them would be circular. They are excluded from the gate metric. The two mr roles (32, 42) and the add (41) share a generic first-def with siblings, so the matcher recovers them partly via use-site; they remain independently distinguishable by def-operand lineage (ig32 copies r3 = the ABI arg; ig42 copies the +44-load base; ig41 adds that base) - a source-grounded distinction not yet in the descriptor, which is why they clear the bar the three identical li-0 zero-inits do not.

same_role_notes.32: mr from r3 (the gobj arg copy)

raw_ig_note: Of the 6 adjudicated roles, raw ig_idx agrees on only ig 32 (matched->wip same number). The other 5 drifted, matching Campaign B's ~1/9 finding. The matcher must recover the drifted roles via identity-core features.
use_site_stress_basis: Use-site-stress labels: GENERIC first-def roles (mr/add/lbz/rlwinm/subf) that DRIFTED, adjudicated by source-grounded composite def-lineage (the chain of defining ops to a unique anchor such as lwz +44 / a named symbol) -- this lineage is the role PRODUCERS, independent of the matcher use_site (consumers) feature, so it is non-circular. Each listed role is recovered by the matcher WITH use_site but NOT when use_site is ablated, proving use_site is load-bearing. The method reproduces mnVibration hand-labels 41->44 and 42->45.
